calendar.matters:
Defendants Ray T. Kelly And Maxine Kelly'S Motion To Set Aside Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-06-2010 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for for hearing on defendants Ray T. Kelly and Maxine Kelly's Motion to Set Aside the Judgment entered March 15, 2010.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Daniel Bornstein, Esq. and Daniel Chong, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Wells Fargo, N.A.
Defendants Ray T. Kelly & Maxine Kelly were present in pro per, placed under oath and examined.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, defendants Ray T. Kelly & Maxine Kelly's Motion to Set Aside the Judgment entered March 15, 2010 is DENIED.

DEFENDANTS RAY KELLY AND MAXINE KELLY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED ON FEBRUARY 18, 2010. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-15-2010 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Prove-Up Hearing. Comm: William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Lois Jones, CSR #3689.

Daniel M. Bornstein, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.
Erin Reed, Asset Manager for plaintiff, was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendants Ray T. Kelly or Maxine Kelly.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was filed on February 25, 2010.

Exhibit--1--3 Day Notice to Quit dated 2/18/10---------------------------------------ID/EVID.
Exhibit--2--Trustee's Deed Upon Sale dated 12/16/09----------------------------------ID/EVID.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A., and against the defendants Ray T. Kelly and Maxine Kelly as follows:

Past-Due Rent & Holdover Damages - $3,833.18.
Costs - Reserved.
Total - $3,833.18.

Additionally, plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. is entitled to Restitution and Possession of the premises located at 60 Gould Street, San Francisco, CA. The lease is forfeited.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

This case did NOT settle.
